title: Campylotropis pinetorum
what_description: Campylotropis pinetorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a climbing legume with a woody stem, typically up to 10 meters long, with elliptical or ovate leaves, 3-8 cm long, 1-3 cm wide, having 2-4 pairs of secondary veins, and a serrated margin. The plant produces small, white or pink flowers, 4-5 mm in diameter, in axillary racemes, with 1-2 flowers per node, and a calyx with 5-6 teeth. The fruit is a flat, narrow pod, 4-7 cm long, 1-2 cm wide, with 2-4 seeds, 2-4 cm long, 1-2 cm wide, and a brown color. It is endemic to China and Taiwan, growing in forests and woodlands.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Campylotropis pinetorum are inflammation and hypertension, which are effectively reduced by its flavonoids and phenolic compounds that inhibit the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and relax blood vessels, thereby lowering blood pressure. It also helps manage diabetes through its ability to stimulate the production of insulin and reduce glucose levels. Additionally, Campylotropis pinetorum has been used to treat fever by inducing sweating and promoting the removal of toxins from the body. Furthermore, it is an effective treatment for arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce joint inflammation and prevent cell damage. Its antibacterial properties also make it a useful remedy for gastroenteritis, and it has been shown to have antitumor activity against certain types of cancer.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Campylotropis pinetorum are its antioxidant capacity, which helps protect the body from oxidative stress. The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of pathogens and preventing infections. Furthermore, the anti-inflammatory properties of Campylotropis pinetorum reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis, and its cardioprotective effects shield the heart from damage caused by high blood pressure and atherosclerosis. Additionally, the plant's analgesic properties alleviate pain by reducing the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, and its bioactive compounds, such as volatile oils, contribute to these medicinal properties. These properties make Campylotropis pinetorum a valuable resource for the development of new medicines and therapies.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Campylotropis pinetorum are flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, and may contribute to the plant's potential in treating various health conditions. These flavonoids have been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, Campylotropis pinetorum contains saponins, which have been reported to exhibit anti-cancer and anti-viral activities, as well as phenolic acids, including caffeic and ferulic acids, that have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The saponins in Campylotropis pinetorum may also contribute to its potential in treating gastrointestinal disorders, as they have been found to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and viruses. Overall, the combination of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids in Campylotropis pinetorum makes it a promising candidate for the development of new medicinal compounds.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Campylotropis pinetorum are infusion and decoction of its leaves and stems which can be used as a diuretic and antipyretic due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Tincture can be prepared from its aerial parts and used to treat various disorders including fever and inflammation. Tea made from the leaves of Campylotropis pinetorum can be used as a treatment for fever and gastrointestinal disorders. Decoction and infusion of its roots can be used for the treatment of various skin infections and wounds. Salve can be prepared from its aerial parts to treat skin injuries and promote wound healing.

Its classification at the genus and species level places it within the Campylotropis genus as its only recognized species.

intro: Campylotropis pinetorum is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its extracts have been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Campylotropis pinetorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and it is also used to make herbal teas, tonics, and extracts that are used to treat a range of health conditions.
